name: Access of Memory Location After End of Buffer
description: The product reads from or writes to a buffer using an index or pointer that references a memory location after the end of the buffer. This typically occurs through pointer incrementation beyond boundaries or arithmetic that positions access past allocated memory.
detection_pattern: INGRESS or TRANSFORM node provides an index or pointer value that flows to a STORAGE node (buffer access) where the computed offset exceeds the buffer end, without a CONTROL node performing upper bounds checking.
